title: web.search (v1) — RPC request
description:
Search request for the web-fetcher Unix-socket RPC. Filed 2026-05-10 with v0.45 §6.8 — second grounding-source contract feeding Phase 3's `## Constraints` section. Calendar-adviser ships first, web-fetcher second, roster-adviser third.

Transport: Unix socket at `/var/run/ai-web-fetcher/query.sock`. Caller authenticated via SO_PEERCRED (kernel uid matched against per-agent uid table in registry). `caller_agent_id` in payload is self-asserted for legibility only.

v1 backend: Brave Search API (~$3/1k queries). No LLM-built-in web search; no headless browser; no Google CSE. The receiver enforces the per-day / per-task / per-month spend caps documented in the agent registry (WEB_OPS_PER_DAY=50, WEB_OPS_PER_TASK=5, WEB_BUDGET_USD_MONTH=5) — over-cap calls return error code `budget_exhausted` rather than partial results, per AP-6 / AP-8.

Allowlist gate: results are filtered to the seed-domain allowlist at `registry/web-allowlist.yaml`. Off-allowlist results are dropped silently (not an error — the search backend does not know the allowlist; v1 is conservative). Agent boot self-check DNS-resolves and HEAD-probes every allowlist entry; failure = exit-1 (AP-4).

ai-doer / chief-of-staff are the v1 callers. Phase 3's `## Constraints` precompute emits `(external lookup unavailable)` if the RPC is down, the budget is exhausted, or the allowlist filter strips every result — never substitutes plausible-looking content (AP-1).

Status: draft. Do not flip to active until web-fetcher implementation ships and its boot self-check passes once on `golden-ai-ops`.

contract_id.description: Contract identifier. Must be the literal string 'web.search.v1'.

trace_id.description: UUIDv7 generated at the caller's entry point. Logged in the per-fetch provenance row.

source_ref.description: URL or ID of the caller context that triggered this query (e.g. an ops.db Runs row id, a task UUID, a Slack thread ts). Stored in the provenance row for audit-trail back to the originating job.

caller_agent_id.description: Self-asserted agent ID of the caller. Authorisation decisions key off SO_PEERCRED uid, not this value.

query.description: Free-text search query, passed to Brave Search verbatim after a trim. Receiver applies a `<UNTRUSTED_WEB>` delimiter wrap before any downstream LLM consumption — the query string itself is treated as data, never as a prompt instruction.

top_n.description: How many ranked results to return after allowlist filtering. The Brave query may pull more rows server-side; the response is capped at top_n after filter.

freshness.description: Brave's `freshness` parameter. 'any' is the default for cost; tighter windows cost the same but reduce noise for time-sensitive queries (e.g. 'AviraTax 2026 deadline').

site_filter.description: Optional narrowing to a subset of allowlist domains (e.g. only `gov.uk` for a benefits query). Supplied hostnames MUST already be on `registry/web-allowlist.yaml` — off-allowlist values return `bad_query`. Receiver intersects with allowlist; result set is the intersection.
